section.para: The written agreements governing the use of digital services do not exist in a legal vacuum. Under California law, the nature of the relationship between a service provider and a user can give rise to duties that cannot be contractually disclaimed. A user may be legally classified as a "patron," triggering a host of specific, non-waivable statutory protections related to safety, fairness, and privacy. In cases where a platform acts as a custodian for a user's data and digital life, it may even assume the role of a "fiduciary," imposing the highest duties of loyalty and care recognized by law. Any attempt to disclaim these foundational duties through one-sided Terms of Service is subject to invalidation under the doctrine of unconscionability, leaving the service provider exposed to liability for breaches of duties they may have believed they had successfully waived.
